title: ప్రహ్లాదుడి ప్రార్థన, నరసింహుడి రాక
moral: నిజమైన నమ్మకం ఉంటే, ఎంత కష్టం వచ్చినా నిన్ను ఎవరూ ఏమీ చేయలేరు కన్నా.
main_character: Prahlada: a young boy (approximately 8-10 years old) with a serene complexion, wearing simple white or pale yellow garments. Hands often folded in prayer (anjali mudra). Calm, unwavering, devotional expression even in the midst of danger — he radiates inner peace. Always the same character across every scene.
antagonist: Hiranyakashipu: A powerful demon king (rakshasa) with a dark, imposing presence and a golden crown. He wears rich, dark garments and elaborate jewelry, always carrying a large mace. His expression is often stern and filled with anger, demanding absolute obedience. Always the same character across every scene.
setting: The grand palace of the Asura king Hiranyakashipu in an ancient kingdom. Carved stone pillars, ornate golden decorations, and sprawling courtyards are visible. The scene transitions between opulent palace interiors and harsh, natural landscapes. Same location across every scene.

scenes.text: మరి అప్పుడు ఏం జరిగిందంటే, చిన్నారి ప్రహ్లాదుడు, తన మెత్తటి తెల్లటి దుస్తుల్లో, రాజభవనం తోటలో కూర్చున్నాడు. అతని కళ్ళు మెల్లగా మూసుకుని ఉన్నాయి, పెదవులపై ఒక చిరునవ్వు మెరిసింది — గుండె నిండా భక్తి నిండినట్లు. మల్లెపూల తీగలు గాలికి మెల్లగా ఊగుతుంటే, వాటి సువాసన అతని చుట్టూ అల్లుకుంది. అతను మెల్లగా జపిస్తున్నాడు: 'నారాయణ! నారాయణ! ఓం నమో నారాయణాయ!' అంతలో, అతను కళ్ళు తెరిచాడు — ఎదురుగా తండ్రి హిరణ్యకశిపుడు నిలబడి ఉన్నాడు, అతని ముఖం చాలా కఠినంగా ఉంది.
scenes.image_prompt: The boy opens his eyes to find his father standing directly in front of him. The father's face is stern and unyielding, contrasting with the boy's soft white clothes. Also in this scene: Hiranyakashipu: A powerful demon king (rakshasa) with a dark, imposing presence and a golden crown. He wears rich, dark garments and elaborate jewelry, always carrying a large mace. His expression is often stern and filled with anger, demanding absolute obedience. Always the same character across every scene. soft, warm morning sunlight filtering through the palace garden leaves, creating dappled light and shadow
scenes.scene_visual: The boy opens his eyes to find his father standing directly in front of him. The father's face is stern and unyielding, contrasting with the boy's soft white clothes.
scenes.text_te_en: Mari Appudu Em Jarigindante, Chinnaari Prahlaadudu, Tana Mettati Tellati Dustullo, Raajabhavanam Totalo Kurchunnaadu. Atani Kallu Mellagaa Musukuni Unnaayi, Pedavulapai Oka Chirunavvu Merisindi — Gunde Nindaa Bhakti Nindinatlu. Mallepula Tigalu Gaaliki Mellagaa Ugutunte, Vaati Suvaasana Atani Chuttu Allukundi. Atanu Mellagaa Japistunnaadu: 'Naaraayana! Naaraayana! Om Namo Naaraayanaaya!' Antalo, Atanu Kallu Terichaadu — Edurugaa Tandri Hiranyakashipudu Nilabadi Unnaadu, Atani Mukham Chaalaa Kathinangaa Undi.
scenes.text_en: And then what happened was, little Prahladudu, in his soft white clothes, sat in the palace garden. His eyes were gently closed, and a smile shone on his lips — as if his heart was full of devotion. As the jasmine vines swayed gently in the breeze, their fragrance enveloped him. He was softly chanting: 'Narayana! Narayana! Om Namo Narayanaya!' Just then, he opened his eyes — his father Hiranyakashipu stood before him, his face very stern.

scenes.text: తండ్రిని చూసినా, ప్రహ్లాదుడి ముఖంలో భయం లేదు — అతని చూపులు స్థిరంగా ఉన్నాయి. హిరణ్యకశిపుడు తన భారీ కళ్ళు పెద్దవి చేసి, గంభీరమైన స్వరంతో అరిచాడు: 'నీవు నన్ను తప్ప ఇంకెవరినీ పూజించకూడదు! నా పేరు మాత్రమే జపించు!' ఆ మాటల ప్రతిధ్వని రాతి గోడలకు తగిలి తిరిగి వస్తున్నప్పుడు, ప్రహ్లాదుడు తల వంచలేదు. అతని చిన్ని భుజాలు నిటారుగా ఉన్నాయి, అతని కళ్ళలో చెక్కుచెదరని నమ్మకం మెరిసింది. ప్రహ్లాదుడి మాట వినకపోవడంతో, హిరణ్యకశిపుడి ముఖం కోపంతో ఎర్రబడింది — అతను కావలి సైనికులకు సైగ చేశాడు.
scenes.image_prompt: The father, his face red with anger, gestures decisively towards his soldiers. The boy stands with small, straight shoulders, his eyes reflecting unwavering faith despite the father's fury. Also in this scene: Hiranyakashipu: A powerful demon king (rakshasa) with a dark, imposing presence and a golden crown. He wears rich, dark garments and elaborate jewelry, always carrying a large mace. His expression is often stern and filled with anger, demanding absolute obedience. Always the same character across every scene. bright, harsh midday sun beating down on the open palace courtyard, casting sharp, defined shadows
scenes.scene_visual: The father, his face red with anger, gestures decisively towards his soldiers. The boy stands with small, straight shoulders, his eyes reflecting unwavering faith despite the father's fury.
scenes.text_te_en: Tandrini Chusinaa, Prahlaadudi Mukhanlo Bhayam Ledu — Atani Chupulu Sthirangaa Unnaayi. Hiranyakashipudu Tana Bhaari Kallu Peddavi Chesi, Gambhiramaina Svaranto Arichaadu: 'Nivu Nannu Tappa Inkevarini Pujinchakudadu! Naa Peru Maatrame Japinchu!' Aa Maatala Pratidhvani Raati Godalaku Tagili Tirigi Vastunnappudu, Prahlaadudu Tala Vanchaledu. Atani Chinni Bhujaalu Nitaarugaa Unnaayi, Atani Kallalo Chekkuchedarani Nammakam Merisindi. Prahlaadudi Maata Vinakapovadanto, Hiranyakashipudi Mukham Kopanto Errabadindi — Atanu Kaavali Sainikulaku Saiga Cheshaadu.
scenes.text_en: Even seeing his father, there was no fear on Prahladudu's face — his gaze was steady. Hiranyakashipu widened his huge eyes and roared in a deep voice: 'You must not worship anyone but me! Chant only my name!' As the echo of those words bounced off the stone walls, Prahladudu did not bow his head. His small shoulders were straight, and unwavering faith shone in his eyes. Because Prahladudu did not listen, Hiranyakashipu's face turned red with anger — he signaled the guard soldiers.

scenes.text: తండ్రి సైగ చేయగానే, సైనికులు ప్రహ్లాదుడిని ఎత్తారు — ఎత్తైన కొండ అంచు వైపు తీసుకెళ్లారు. అతను కిందకు విసిరివేయబడినప్పుడు, గాలి అతని చెవుల గుండా వేగంగా దూసుకుపోయింది — కానీ అతని ముఖం ప్రశాంతంగా ఉంది. ప్రహ్లాదుడు కళ్ళు మూసుకున్నాడు, పెదవులు కదిలాయి: 'నాకు ఏ భయము లేదు, నా హృదయంలో విష్ణువు ఉన్నారు.' కొండ అంచు నుండి చూస్తున్న హిరణ్యకశిపుడు తన కళ్ళు పెద్దవి చేసుకున్నాడు — ఒక్కసారిగా — గాలిలో ఆగిపోయాడు. ఒక అదృశ్య శక్తి అతన్ని పట్టుకుని, మెత్తగా భూమిపైకి చేర్చింది — ఏ చిరుగాయం కూడా లేకుండా. హిరణ్యకశిపుడికి నోట మాట రాలేదు, అతని కళ్ళు ఆశ్చర్యంతో నిండిపోయాయి.
scenes.image_prompt: The boy hangs suspended in the air after being thrown from the cliff's edge. His face remains peaceful, eyes closed, as his lips move in silent prayer while his father watches in wide-eyed astonishment. Also in this scene: Hiranyakashipu: A powerful demon king (rakshasa) with a dark, imposing presence and a golden crown. He wears rich, dark garments and elaborate jewelry, always carrying a large mace. His expression is often stern and filled with anger, demanding absolute obedience. Always the same character across every scene. bright, clear daylight at the top of a towering cliff, with a hazy, distant view of the plains below
scenes.scene_visual: The boy hangs suspended in the air after being thrown from the cliff's edge. His face remains peaceful, eyes closed, as his lips move in silent prayer while his father watches in wide-eyed astonishment.
scenes.text_te_en: Tandri Saiga Cheyagaane, Sainikulu Prahlaadudini Ettaaru — Ettaina Konda Anchu Vaipu Tisukellaaru. Atanu Kindaku Visiriveyabadinappudu, Gaali Atani Chevula Gundaa Vegangaa Dusukupoyindi — Kaani Atani Mukham Prashaantangaa Undi. Prahlaadudu Kallu Musukunnaadu, Pedavulu Kadilaayi: 'Naaku E Bhayamu Ledu, Naa Hrridayanlo Vishnuvu Unnaaru.' Konda Anchu Nundi Chustunna Hiranyakashipudu Tana Kallu Peddavi Chesukunnaadu — Okkasaarigaa — Gaalilo Aagipoyaadu. Oka Adrrishya Shakti Atanni Pattukuni, Mettagaa Bhumipaiki Cherchindi — E Chirugaayam Kudaa Lekundaa. Hiranyakashipudiki Nota Maata Raaledu, Atani Kallu Aashcharyanto Nindipoyaayi.
scenes.text_en: As soon as his father signaled, the soldiers picked up Prahladudu — and took him towards the edge of a high cliff. When he was thrown down, the wind rushed past his ears — but his face was peaceful. Prahladudu closed his eyes, his lips moved: 'I have no fear, Vishnu is in my heart.' Hiranyakashipu, watching from the cliff edge, widened his eyes — all of a sudden — Prahladudu stopped in mid-air. An invisible force caught him and gently placed him on the ground — without even a small scratch. Hiranyakashipu was speechless, his eyes were filled with wonder.

scenes.text: ప్రహ్లాదుడు మళ్ళీ నిలబడ్డాడు, అతని ముఖంపై అదే చిరునవ్వు — ఏమీ జరగనట్లే. హిరణ్యకశిపుడు ఎన్ని రకాలుగా ప్రయత్నించినా, ప్రహ్లాదుడికి ఏమీ చేయలేకపోయాడు. ఒకసారి, ఒక పెద్ద ఏనుగును అతనిపైకి పంపాడు, కానీ ఆ ఏనుగు తొండం — ప్రేమగా నిమిరింది. ఆ ఏనుగు గందరగోళంగా గుసగుసలాడుతూ, ప్రహ్లాదుడిని తన పక్కకు తోసింది. అది చూసి ప్రహ్లాదుడు 'నా ప్రభువు నన్ను ఎల్లప్పుడూ రక్షిస్తారు' అని శాంతంగా అన్నాడు. హిరణ్యకశిపుడికి కోపం కట్టలు తెంచుకుంది, అతని వేళ్ళు పిడికిలి బిగించాయి — రాజమందిరంలోని ఒక భారీ రాతి స్తంభం వైపు వేలు చూపాడు.
scenes.image_prompt: The father, his fingers clenched into a fist, furiously points towards a massive stone pillar in the royal hall. The boy stands calmly beside a large elephant that has gently nudged him, a serene smile on his face. Also in this scene: Hiranyakashipu: A powerful demon king (rakshasa) with a dark, imposing presence and a golden crown. He wears rich, dark garments and elaborate jewelry, always carrying a large mace. His expression is often stern and filled with anger, demanding absolute obedience. Always the same character across every scene. dim, cavernous lighting in a palace dungeon, with only a few torches casting flickering, orange light
scenes.scene_visual: The father, his fingers clenched into a fist, furiously points towards a massive stone pillar in the royal hall. The boy stands calmly beside a large elephant that has gently nudged him, a serene smile on his face.
scenes.text_te_en: Prahlaadudu Malli Nilabaddaadu, Atani Mukhampai Ade Chirunavvu — Emi Jaraganatle. Hiranyakashipudu Enni Rakaalugaa Prayatninchinaa, Prahlaadudiki Emi Cheyalekapoyaadu. Okasaari, Oka Pedda Enugunu Atanipaiki Pampaadu, Kaani Aa Enugu Tondam — Premagaa Nimirindi. Aa Enugu Gandaragolangaa Gusagusalaadutu, Prahlaadudini Tana Pakkaku Tosindi. Adi Chusi Prahlaadudu 'Naa Prabhuvu Nannu Ellappudu Rakshistaaru' Ani Shaantangaa Annaadu. Hiranyakashipudiki Kopam Kattalu Tenchukundi, Atani Vellu Pidikili Biginchaayi — Raajamandiranloni Oka Bhaari Raati Stambham Vaipu Velu Chupaadu.
scenes.text_en: Prahladudu stood up again, with the same smile on his face — as if nothing had happened. No matter how many ways Hiranyakashipu tried, he could not harm Prahladudu. Once, he sent a big elephant towards him, but the elephant's trunk — gently caressed him. The elephant, trumpeting in confusion, nudged Prahladudu to its side. Seeing that, Prahladudu calmly said, 'My Lord always protects me.' Hiranyakashipu's anger burst forth, his fingers clenched into fists — he pointed a finger towards a huge stone pillar in the royal palace.

scenes.text: హిరణ్యకశిపుడి వేలు ఆ స్తంభం వైపు చూపగానే, ప్రహ్లాదుడు ఆ స్తంభాన్ని చూశాడు — అతని కళ్ళలో ఒక మెరుపు. 'నీ దేవుడు అంతటా ఉంటాడంటావు కదా, మరి ఈ స్తంభంలో కూడా ఉన్నాడా?' అని హిరణ్యకశిపుడు వెటకారంగా అడిగాడు, అతని మాటల్లో పదును. ఆ పెద్ద హాల్లో ప్రాచీన రాతి వాసన ఘాటుగా అలుముకుంది, ప్రహ్లాదుడి స్వరంలో ఎలాంటి భయం లేదు. అతను చిన్నగా, కానీ స్పష్టంగా అన్నాడు: 'అతను ప్రతిచోటా ఉన్నాడు, ఈ స్తంభంలో కూడా!' ప్రహ్లాదుడి మాట వినగానే, హిరణ్యకశిపుడి కనుబొమ్మలు ముడిపడ్డాయి — అతను తన గదను పైకెత్తాడు. ఆ కోపంతో, హిరణ్యకశిపుడు ఆ భారీ స్తంభాన్ని తన గదతో బలంగా కొట్టాడు.
scenes.image_prompt: The father raises his mace high and brings it down with immense force onto the massive stone pillar. The boy watches with a flash of understanding in his eyes, standing fearlessly in the ancient stone hall. Also in this scene: Hiranyakashipu: A powerful demon king (rakshasa) with a dark, imposing presence and a golden crown. He wears rich, dark garments and elaborate jewelry, always carrying a large mace. His expression is often stern and filled with anger, demanding absolute obedience. Always the same character across every scene. grand, regal lighting in the main palace hall, with bright lamps illuminating golden decorations and tapestries
scenes.scene_visual: The father raises his mace high and brings it down with immense force onto the massive stone pillar. The boy watches with a flash of understanding in his eyes, standing fearlessly in the ancient stone hall.
scenes.text_te_en: Hiranyakashipudi Velu Aa Stambham Vaipu Chupagaane, Prahlaadudu Aa Stambhaanni Chushaadu — Atani Kallalo Oka Merupu. 'Ni Devudu Antataa Untaadantaavu Kadaa, Mari I Stambhanlo Kudaa Unnaadaa?' Ani Hiranyakashipudu Vetakaarangaa Adigaadu, Atani Maatallo Padunu. Aa Pedda Haallo Praachina Raati Vaasana Ghaatugaa Alumukundi, Prahlaadudi Svaranlo Elaanti Bhayam Ledu. Atanu Chinnagaa, Kaani Spashtangaa Annaadu: 'Atanu Pratichotaa Unnaadu, I Stambhanlo Kudaa!' Prahlaadudi Maata Vinagaane, Hiranyakashipudi Kanubommalu Mudipaddaayi — Atanu Tana Gadanu Paikettaadu. Aa Kopanto, Hiranyakashipudu Aa Bhaari Stambhaanni Tana Gadato Balangaa Kottaadu.
scenes.text_en: As soon as Hiranyakashipu's finger pointed towards the pillar, Prahladudu looked at the pillar — a sparkle in his eyes. 'You say your God is everywhere, so is he in this pillar too?' Hiranyakashipu asked sarcastically, with sharpness in his words. The strong smell of ancient stone filled the large hall, but there was no fear in Prahladudu's voice. He said softly, but clearly: 'He is everywhere, even in this pillar!' As soon as he heard Prahladudu's words, Hiranyakashipu's eyebrows furrowed — he raised his mace. In his anger, Hiranyakashipu struck the huge pillar forcefully with his mace.

scenes.text: హిరణ్యకశిపుడి గద ఆ స్తంభానికి తగలగానే, ఆ స్తంభం — ఒక్కసారిగా చీలిపోయింది! ఒక మెరిసే కాంతి, భూమిని కదిలించే ఒక పెద్ద గర్జనతో, ఆ స్తంభం నుండి ఒక అద్భుత రూపం బయటపడింది. అది చూడగానే ప్రహ్లాదుడు మోకాళ్ళపై వాలాడు, అతని కళ్ళ నుండి ఆనంద భాష్పాలు ధారగా కారాయి. అతను 'ప్రభువు వచ్చారు! నా ప్రభువు వచ్చారు!' అని కేక పెట్టాడు, అతని గొంతులో భక్తి నిండి ఉంది. ఆ దివ్య శక్తి ఆ రాజమందిరాన్ని నింపేసింది, చెడు శక్తి అక్కడ నిలబడలేకపోయింది. ఆ క్షణంలో, న్యాయం జరిగింది, భక్తుడి ప్రార్థన ఫలించింది.
scenes.image_prompt: A magnificent divine form bursts forth from the massive stone pillar as it splits apart with a rumbling roar. The boy immediately falls to his knees, tears of joy streaming from his eyes, his voice filled with devotion. Also in this scene: Hiranyakashipu: A powerful demon king (rakshasa) with a dark, imposing presence and a golden crown. He wears rich, dark garments and elaborate jewelry, always carrying a large mace. His expression is often stern and filled with anger, demanding absolute obedience. Always the same character across every scene. SUPPORTING CHARACTER also in this scene: Narasimha: a fierce deity with a lion's head (golden mane, bared teeth) on a powerful human body. Multiple arms holding weapons including the Sudarshana Chakra. Wearing royal ornaments; body radiating divine energy. Expression: ferocious toward demons, protective toward devotees. a sudden, overwhelming burst of brilliant golden-white divine radiance emanating from the pillar, making everything else seem dim and shadowy
scenes.scene_visual: A magnificent divine form bursts forth from the massive stone pillar as it splits apart with a rumbling roar. The boy immediately falls to his knees, tears of joy streaming from his eyes, his voice filled with devotion.
scenes.text_te_en: Hiranyakashipudi Gada Aa Stambhaaniki Tagalagaane, Aa Stambham — Okkasaarigaa Chilipoyindi! Oka Merise Kaanti, Bhumini Kadilinche Oka Pedda Garjanato, Aa Stambham Nundi Oka Adbhuta Rupam Bayatapadindi. Adi Chudagaane Prahlaadudu Mokaallapai Vaalaadu, Atani Kalla Nundi Aananda Bhaashpaalu Dhaaragaa Kaaraayi. Atanu 'Prabhuvu Vachchaaru! Naa Prabhuvu Vachchaaru!' Ani Keka Pettaadu, Atani Gontulo Bhakti Nindi Undi. Aa Divya Shakti Aa Raajamandiraanni Nimpesindi, Chedu Shakti Akkada Nilabadalekapoyindi. Aa Kshananlo, Nyaayam Jarigindi, Bhaktudi Praarthana Phalinchindi.
scenes.text_en: As soon as Hiranyakashipu's mace hit the pillar, the pillar — suddenly split open! With a shining light and a mighty roar that shook the earth, a wondrous form emerged from the pillar. As soon as he saw it, Prahladudu fell to his knees, and tears of joy streamed from his eyes. He cried out, 'The Lord has come! My Lord has come!' his voice filled with devotion. That divine power filled the royal palace, and evil power could not stand there. In that moment, justice was served, and the devotee's prayer was answered.

scenes.text: ఆ దివ్య రూపం నిలిచిన చోట, ఒక ప్రశాంతమైన వెలుగు నిండింది — భయం అంతా మాయమైంది. పవిత్రమైన పూల సువాసన గాలిలో మెల్లగా తేలియాడుతోంది, ఒక మధురమైన అనుభూతి చుట్టూ అల్లుకుంది. ప్రహ్లాదుడు మెల్లగా లేచి నిలబడ్డాడు, అతని ముఖంపై ఒక దివ్యమైన శాంతి — అతని హృదయం కృతజ్ఞతతో నిండి ఉంది. అతను మళ్ళీ చేతులు జోడించి, మెల్లగా 'ఓం నమో నారాయణాయ!' అని జపించాడు. అలా ప్రహ్లాదుడి భక్తి ప్రపంచానికి ఒక గొప్ప పాఠంగా నిలిచింది — నిజమైన నమ్మకానికి సాటి లేదని. ఆ రాజ్యానికి శాంతి తిరిగి వచ్చింది, ప్రహ్లాదుడు తన ప్రేమను, భక్తిని పంచుకుంటూ జీవించాడు.
scenes.image_prompt: The boy slowly rises to his feet, joining his hands together in a gesture of prayer. A divine peace radiates from his face, illuminated by the serene light filling the space, as he softly chants. soft, warm, golden light filling the now peaceful palace hall, suggesting a new dawn of tranquility
scenes.scene_visual: The boy slowly rises to his feet, joining his hands together in a gesture of prayer. A divine peace radiates from his face, illuminated by the serene light filling the space, as he softly chants.
scenes.text_te_en: Aa Divya Rupam Nilichina Chota, Oka Prashaantamaina Velugu Nindindi — Bhayam Antaa Maayamaindi. Pavitramaina Pula Suvaasana Gaalilo Mellagaa Teliyaadutondi, Oka Madhuramaina Anubhuti Chuttu Allukundi. Prahlaadudu Mellagaa Lechi Nilabaddaadu, Atani Mukhampai Oka Divyamaina Shaanti — Atani Hrridayam Krritajnyatato Nindi Undi. Atanu Malli Chetulu Jodinchi, Mellagaa 'Om Namo Naaraayanaaya!' Ani Japinchaadu. Alaa Prahlaadudi Bhakti Prapanchaaniki Oka Goppa Paathangaa Nilichindi — Nijamaina Nammakaaniki Saati Ledani. Aa Raajyaaniki Shaanti Tirigi Vachchindi, Prahlaadudu Tana Premanu, Bhaktini Panchukuntu Jivinchaadu.
scenes.text_en: Where that divine form stood, a peaceful light filled the space — all fear vanished. The fragrance of sacred flowers gently floated in the air, and a sweet feeling enveloped everything. Prahladudu slowly stood up, with a divine peace on his face — his heart was full of gratitude. He again joined his hands and softly chanted 'Om Namo Narayanaya!' Thus, Prahladudu's devotion became a great lesson to the world — that true faith has no equal. Peace returned to that kingdom, and Prahladudu lived sharing his love and devotion.

quality_scores.notes: This story shines with its authentic oral storytelling voice and a compelling narrative arc that keeps the reader engaged. The grammar is flawless, making it a joy to read. To reach an award-worthy level, the author should focus on consistently *showing* emotions through character actions and reactions, rather than explicitly stating them. Additionally, enhancing the dialogue to be present in every scene and giving each character a more distinct voice would significantly enrich the story.

moral_te_en: Nijamaina Nammakam Unte, Enta Kashtam Vachchinaa Ninnu Evaru Emi Cheyaleru Kannaa.
moral_en: My dear, if you have true faith, no matter how much trouble comes, no one can harm you.
